title: The effects of sham hypnosis techniques

affiliations.name: Institute of Psychology ELTE, Eötvös Lorand University  Budapest Hungary

funding_references.funder_name: Hungarian National Research, Development and Innovation Office

description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 52 participants (39 females) investigating the neurophysiological effects of sham hypnosis techniques using a 2×2 balanced placebo design. Participants underwent four experimental conditions involving conventional hypnotic inductions and unconventional (placebo) inductions, each presented as either hypnosis or relaxation control, with pre- and post-intervention baseline recordings. Brain electrophysiological activity was recorded using 64-channel EEG to examine how expectancy and framing influence neural responses to hypnotic inductions.
methods_description: EEG data were collected using a 64-channel EasyCap system from Brain Products. The study protocol consisted of a 5-minute closed-eyes baseline, four experimental blocks with different hypnotic induction conditions presented in randomized order within a 2×2 balanced placebo design, and a final 5-minute closed-eyes baseline.
